textDisplay: I’ll most likely be the outlier here. This trailer looks like they both resurrected and killed the Ghostbusters series in a single film. The old movie model use to work, and they should have stuck with it… new film, new ghost boss and plot… simple and everyone enjoyed it. The CG animation looks horrible, and it’s like they over-salted dinner with how much they tried to add into one film. I hope I’m wrong, because I’d love to see a proper Ghostbusters film, and the series deserves it.
textOriginal: I’ll most likely be the outlier here. This trailer looks like they both resurrected and killed the Ghostbusters series in a single film. The old movie model use to work, and they should have stuck with it… new film, new ghost boss and plot… simple and everyone enjoyed it. The CG animation looks horrible, and it’s like they over-salted dinner with how much they tried to add into one film. I hope I’m wrong, because I’d love to see a proper Ghostbusters film, and the series deserves it.

textDisplay:
Well, I'll watch it. Haven't seen 3 or 4,  2 wasn't great - but it has the original cast and is supposed to be good. Will report back here with comments in a few weeks ..
===========
Edit: Finally watched it. It's actually pretty good. They've used the original cast very tastefully (it's not full of Bill Murray) and the new cast works really well, especially Mckenna Grace as Phoebe with Emily Alyn Lind as Melody. The story is engaging without being corny or trying too hard. It just works. I can't believe James Acaster is in it as the science geek as well! It's way better than I expected, pleasantly surprised, cheers to the makers. 🙂

textDisplay:
I was born in 1984 and when I was growing up in the 80s and early 90s I lived and breathed this franchise, I had all the toys, made proton packs and outfits from cereal packets and anything else I had lying about the house, and pretended to drive to ghost calls in my kettler go kart which served as my Ecto-1. 

I watched the Real Ghostbusters everyday after school and dreamt of being a Ghostbuster myself when I grew up. Now I’m a 40 year old Fire-fighter (closest job I could get to being a Ghostbuster 😂) and me and my partner are doing our first visit to NY from Scotland in two weeks, where we will get to watch the new movie set there , in the city where it all began, I am literally excited beyond words and feel like that excited 5 year old kid waiting to go and see GB2 in the cinema again like I did in 89! 

To see what is being done with this franchise really fills me with nostalgic joy, and I’m so happy to see the old team being shared with the new generations and also the new team that has been assembled, the casting is absolutley solid, I’m sure Ivan and Harold would be proud to see what is being done with the franchise now and the respect that it is being shown 👍
textOriginal:
I was born in 1984 and when I was growing up in the 80s and early 90s I lived and breathed this franchise, I had all the toys, made proton packs and outfits from cereal packets and anything else I had lying about the house, and pretended to drive to ghost calls in my kettler go kart which served as my Ecto-1. 

I watched the Real Ghostbusters everyday after school and dreamt of being a Ghostbuster myself when I grew up. Now I’m a 40 year old Fire-fighter (closest job I could get to being a Ghostbuster 😂) and me and my partner are doing our first visit to NY from Scotland in two weeks, where we will get to watch the new movie set there , in the city where it all began, I am literally excited beyond words and feel like that excited 5 year old kid waiting to go and see GB2 in the cinema again like I did in 89! 

To see what is being done with this franchise really fills me with nostalgic joy, and I’m so happy to see the old team being shared with the new generations and also the new team that has been assembled, the casting is absolutley solid, I’m sure Ivan and Harold would be proud to see what is being done with the franchise now and the respect that it is being shown 👍

textDisplay:
​@TheMeatwade Trust man i didn't not take it personally I just thought your comment was funny lol

I just think your reading into things wayyyy too much thinking that Hollywood remakes and entertainment companies are going to cause the collapse of a civilization. I'm actually in studying for a history major actually right now, and trust our civilization has gone through a lot worse than large company corporations holding all the money. Hell 50 rich people running a country is always how humanity has run. But times are different now and the entertainment that you say is "blinding people to the fall" is actually what is informing people of the fall. 

Entertainment is used as junk food for corporations to make a shit ton of money off ad's and customer demand of course, but social media is a way that the forward thinking of humanity have been able to put to use to rally people together against these corporations. Which is why there is such a big push to uproot these monopolies nowadays. Civilizations can't simply just fall as easily they did long ago, hell I would say that the only way any 1st world country can fall nowadays is a world-ending scenario or a third world war.

Anyways none of this is meant to come across as rude, just using it as practice for my English class next semester, cheers!

textDisplay: I feel as much attachment (sometimes even more) for Ray, Venkman, Spengler (RIP), Winston, Janine, Louis and even Dana Barrett as I do for some of my family members, so this opportunity to share a new adventure with some of them after so many years makes me a little more emotional than it should. I feel like shouting out to them: I love you guys!

textDisplay: When i was 15 I began work in special effects, my mentor worked for Boss Films. He was kind enough to give me a piece of the original Demon Dog statues that was smashed to chunks by kids in an alley just after Ghostbusters finished the shots with it. That may sound dumb keeping that piece, but seeing this, knowing that it's not just a film series but a living thing makes me happy to have been given that. These films are gifts to hold and pass on as pieces of us all. I respectfully tip my hat to each and every one of you that have kept it alive and well.
textOriginal: When i was 15 I began work in special effects, my mentor worked for Boss Films. He was kind enough to give me a piece of the original Demon Dog statues that was smashed to chunks by kids in an alley just after Ghostbusters finished the shots with it. That may sound dumb keeping that piece, but seeing this, knowing that it's not just a film series but a living thing makes me happy to have been given that. These films are gifts to hold and pass on as pieces of us all. I respectfully tip my hat to each and every one of you that have kept it alive and well.

textDisplay:
Sigh. This has every ingredient of what you shouldn’t do when making a sequel.

Repeating iconic lines for no apparent reason (including, in this case, a line from the film’s theme song. Which is utterly bizarre).

It basically looks like a bunch of studio execs sat in the room and thought “what were all the stuff they liked from the 80s film? …just throw it all at them again.”

Is there actually any point to this film other than try and make a load of money off the worst kind of nostalgia?

Tiresome.
textOriginal:
Sigh. This has every ingredient of what you shouldn’t do when making a sequel.

Repeating iconic lines for no apparent reason (including, in this case, a line from the film’s theme song. Which is utterly bizarre).

It basically looks like a bunch of studio execs sat in the room and thought “what were all the stuff they liked from the 80s film? …just throw it all at them again.”

Is there actually any point to this film other than try and make a load of money off the worst kind of nostalgia?

Tiresome.

textDisplay:
What gets me excited for the look of this, even more than Afterlife (which was OK, but I think this looks quite a bit better):

The original cast look like they're going to play a bigger part, and not just as cheap nostalgia. The sense of threat is pretty high, like the original, even though we know there's going to be laughs too. 

Take the last Slimer shot - it takes Slimer kind of seriously. It's funny too, as it was when Ray got slimed in the original, but the undercurrent is kind of serious - notice the sighing 'yeah' - which is cool, because it's acknowledging the threat. Remember the first one was scary, alongside the giggles.

The shots of people being shattered in two as ice, and the new buster girl with a proton pack possibly near death as she's freezing, sold it.

Boy oh boy, am I looking forward to this in the cinema, reviews permitting.
textOriginal:
What gets me excited for the look of this, even more than Afterlife (which was OK, but I think this looks quite a bit better):

Take the last Slimer shot - it takes Slimer kind of seriously. It's funny too, as it was when Ray got slimed in the original, but the undercurrent is kind of serious - notice the sighing 'yeah' - which is cool, because it's acknowledging the threat. Remember the first one was scary, alongside the giggles.

The shots of people being shattered in two as ice, and the new buster girl with a proton pack possibly near death as she's freezing, sold it.

Boy oh boy, am I looking forward to this in the cinema, reviews permitting.

textDisplay: I was 8, and I can still remember when my entire class went to see the first Ghostbusters at a matinee. We were scared silly...much later I came to appreciate the "silly" more the the "scared" and recognized all the dark humor I'd missed as a kid. Ghostbusters: Afterlife was a different kind of cinematic experience, but it paid homage to the original guys (and Harold Ramis) and so moved me, in my middle age with so much happening with sick family members, that we ended up seeing it twice. It was a great pick-me-up and a nice start to a new chapter in the franchise! Can't wait for this one, although now I wish I hadn't seen the trailer 'cause they put so much in it. Will be careful not to show it to the wife. (LOL)
textOriginal: I was 8, and I can still remember when my entire class went to see the first Ghostbusters at a matinee. textDisplay:
Ghostbusters was one of the three greatest films of the 80's, along with Back to the Future and Gremlins. A huge part of my childhood. I must have seen it scores of times. The second was decent but, after all, pale in comparison to the original. These days, Hollywood lacks originality, and they go back and reboot 30-year-old films or decide to make sequels to classic films that are really untouchable, and then they have to through in all sorts of nostalgia to pique the interest of the original fans--in Afterlife, Gozer and Stay Puft. (I need not say what they did with the 9th Star Wars film, bringing back every character from every SW movie imaginable.) Hard enough to watch a Ghostbusters film without Rick Moranis, a Ghostbusters film without Harold Ramis is like a Beatles concert without John, so I've passed on Afterlife until now.
I see this trailer, though, and I'm thinking this may be the Ghostbusters sequel I needed, so I am tempted.

Gremlins 3, by the way, I await with anticipation. If they do a direct sequel and not some crummy reboot, I'm totally on board with the next Gremlins.
textOriginal:
Ghostbusters was one of the three greatest films of the 80's, along with Back to the Future and Gremlins. A huge part of my childhood. I must have seen it scores of times. The second was decent but, after all, pale in comparison to the original. These days, Hollywood lacks originality, and they go back and reboot 30-year-old films or decide to make sequels to classic films that are really untouchable, and then they have to through in all sorts of nostalgia to pique the interest of the original fans--in Afterlife, Gozer and Stay Puft. (I need not say what they did with the 9th Star Wars film, bringing back every character from every SW movie imaginable.) Hard enough to watch a Ghostbusters film without Rick Moranis, a Ghostbusters film without Harold Ramis is like a Beatles concert without John, so I've passed on Afterlife until now.
I see this trailer, though, and I'm thinking this may be the Ghostbusters sequel I needed, so I am tempted.

Gremlins 3, by the way, I await with anticipation. If they do a direct sequel and not some crummy reboot, I'm totally on board with the next Gremlins.

textDisplay:
People keep talking shit about this movie, like it's gonna be bad. I don't know. 
Looks fun to me.
Weird. The guy that played that EPA guy (I think it was) in the first movie is back. I thought I read that he passed away? Alright. Im glad he's there too.
The only thing missing is Rick Moranis.

I will say, though. My one problem with these movies is the meme-ing. The constant referencing the past movies. Like someone in universe, even if he delivers it very well, should not say "Bustin' makes me feel good." Are they saying now that song was diegetic? There's another song no one remembers from the sequel that was, but that song was not. Its very weird. Maybe that's just for the trailer, but the amount of Egon cock gobbling in the first movie was pretty wild. I get it for the first movie. They had to get past the terrible 2016 movie. Let's move on now from the constant references, and let's let this movie now prove that sequels are even necessary. Let's tell a new Ghostbusters story. You're being faithful so what else you got now beyond "Remember... remember... remember that thing you liked?"

textDisplay: Sorry, I have to BUST your bubble but how much can remix and reboot a movie? This could even be a mash-up. This is not a true sequal in any sense of the word. They're repeating everything from the previous movie and the original two with the same cast. Sony Pictures are basically like we're going to keep doing this every 5 years. There's more screen time on the old actors' corny jokes than the villain.

textDisplay:
Though unlike some people, I did like the last one, I admit to feeling this one will be a lot better. Instead of just having a bunch of SNL comedians, OF TODAY that is, which made the other feel more like a silly parody/SNL sketch, this one seems more like an actual part of the franchise more in an official way, even if Bill Murray did do the other one too! 

Again I did enjoy the other one too, what made it not hold up to the others wasn't because they had an all female lead cast, it was the writing and like I said, it was done more like a SNL sketch, then a serious contender of the original or another chapter or whatever. Just from the trailer this doesn't feel that way, with better actors in it and a lot more of the original cast, it fits better as a sequel or continuance to the originals! 

But I hate that I really want to see it March 22, but I don't go out to the theater anymore, having in more then 20 years, I'll just have to wait until it comes on cable or streaming, well cable only if it gets on HBO, since i have MAX! Luckily it doesn't take as long for that to happen as it used to! 😉
textOriginal:
Though unlike some people, I did like the last one, I admit to feeling this one will be a lot better. Instead of just having a bunch of SNL comedians, OF TODAY that is, which made the other feel more like a silly parody/SNL sketch, this one seems more like an actual part of the franchise more in an official way, even if Bill Murray did do the other one too! 

Again I did enjoy the other one too, what made it not hold up to the others wasn't because they had an all female lead cast, it was the writing and like I said, it was done more like a SNL sketch, then a serious contender of the original or another chapter or whatever. Just from the trailer this doesn't feel that way, with better actors in it and a lot more of the original cast, it fits better as a sequel or continuance to the originals! 

But I hate that I really want to see it March 22, but I don't go out to the theater anymore, having in more then 20 years, I'll just have to wait until it comes on cable or streaming, well cable only if it gets on HBO, since i have MAX! Luckily it doesn't take as long for that to happen as it used to! 😉

textDisplay:
I am really excited for this. This is what I wanted from the first one - an original ghostbuster story. 

Callbacks are great, but did it have to be Gozer? The story could've worked with just about any baddie. "Ray - Egon was obsessed....we stopped Gozer, but the keymaster and gatekeeper got together and the gates were open for hours. Who knows what made it through." or "Shando was into all sorts of weird paranormal activity. Gozer was just one of many gods he worshipped."

From the trailers I think they are eliminating the only gripe I had with the first one. Don't get me wrong, I did still enjoy it though.

textDisplay: My biggest problem with Afterlife was that, for all the references and call backs to the events of the original movie, I can't recall them making a single Ghostbusters 2 reference. You know, the movie where they made the Statue of Liberty WALK. People in New York would remember that for decades, and there's no way footage of it wasn't also being broadcast to the rest of the World while that was happening. GB 2 is a great movie that just couldn't quite live up to its predecessor. But, considering how high GB 1 set the bar, surpassing it in quality would've been virtually impossible. I'm keeping my fingers crossed that this new movie will show GB 2 the respect it deserves, especially now that the story is back in New York where the Ghostbusters should never have been forgotten.
textOriginal: My biggest problem with Afterlife was that, for all the references and call backs to the events of the original movie, I can't recall them making a single Ghostbusters 2 reference. You know, the movie where they made the Statue of Liberty WALK. People in New York would remember that for decades, and there's no way footage of it wasn't also being broadcast to the rest of the World while that was happening. GB 2 is a great movie that just couldn't quite live up to its predecessor. But, considering how high GB 1 set the bar, surpassing it in quality would've been virtually impossible. I'm keeping my fingers crossed that this new movie will show GB 2 the respect it deserves, especially now that the story is back in New York where the Ghostbusters should never have been forgotten.

textDisplay: All lives are precious so in any situation the gospel is needed: "For God so loved the world that He gave his only Son that whosoever believes in Him will not perish but have eternal life." (John 3:16) God is just so because we broke his moral law by doing wrong against Him, ourselves and others, His punishment for sin is the death penalty. However He is also merciful which is why He gave Himself in human form (Jesus) to die for us, and take away our sins if we repent and believe in Him. If we do, our debt or bail is paid and we can go free. God is also merciful for those who have never heard the gospel and will judge them by their conscience, actions and belief in Him from evidence of creation.

textDisplay: The first film was iconic, there was a significant story, that they made make sense. The second lost its charm and by the end was just plain dumb. The third movie relied strictly on nostalgia, hoping to get back audiences and a story that had plot holes big enough to drive a MAC truck through. This movie feels like it should have been the third. I'll see it, to say I saw it, but they are getting a bit too Nickelodeon for me.

The third movie relied strictly on nostalgia, hoping to get back audiences and a story that had plot holes big enough to drive a MAC truck through. This movie feels like it should have been the third. I'll see it, to say I saw it, but they are getting a bit too Nickelodeon for me.

textDisplay: I can’t wait to see this movie, I love Ghostbusters so much but there’s one thing that the last couple canon movies in this movie having common that I think is a mistake in the writing, and that’s this “there were no witnesses so the Ghostbusters obviously made up all these things” like even in Ghostbusters two like in the first five minutes everyone in New York thinks they’re liars, but there were millions of people that saw what happened that night with Gozzer and I’m sure that it was covered in the news lol in Ghostbusters afterlife you even see them watching YouTube clips of the things that happened. Then at the end of Ghostbusters 2 the events unfold with the entire city in the streets watching everything . Why why do they keep writing lines that would indicate that all this happened in a haunted house in the middle of Kansas somewhere where nobody saw anything? It’s just a continuity thing that bugs me, but man I cannot wait to see this movie!
textOriginal: I can’t wait to see this movie, I love Ghostbusters so much but there’s one thing that the last couple canon movies in this movie having common that I think is a mistake in the writing, and that’s this “there were no witnesses so the Ghostbusters obviously made up all these things” like even in Ghostbusters two like in the first five minutes everyone in New York thinks they’re liars, but there were millions of people that saw what happened that night with Gozzer and I’m sure that it was covered in the news lol in Ghostbusters afterlife you even see them watching YouTube clips of the things that happened. Then at the end of Ghostbusters 2 the events unfold with the entire city in the streets watching everything . Why why do they keep writing lines that would indicate that all this happened in a haunted house in the middle of Kansas somewhere where nobody saw anything? It’s just a continuity thing that bugs me, but man I cannot wait to see this movie!

textDisplay:
OMG, this is going to be the perfect follow-up to Ghostbusters:  Afterlife.  Janine is going to suit up and join the team for the first time in the movies (she was a Ghostbuster on occasions in Real Ghostbusters), Walter Peck looks like he is now mayor of NYC, which will possibly allow him to intensify his feud with the Ghostbusters, and we can see the return of the Mini-Puffs, and even some of the iconic ghosts, like the Librarian Ghost, and the grand return of Slimer.

I dunno if Slimer can read comments, but if he can, I want to welcome him back, as I missed the green spud.

textOriginal:
My boys are back and have backup now. Been a Ghostbusters fan since I was born in 1984 and haven't stopped loving them. Glad Afterlife did so well that we got more Ghostbusters films. 
I was hoping the Ecto-1 would've gotten an upgrade for Frozen Empire like it did in Ghostbusters II, but, still excited to see the Ecto-1 back in Manhattan.
0:23, the Ghostbusters II logo on the box......FINALLY! Hopefully we get to see the grey uniforms and slime throwers return too.

textDisplay:
Ghostbusters was 2 fun comedies from the 80's. it doesn't need a franchise. but if they insist on making these "super epic" sequels they gotta let go of the past and make it t's own thing. Afterlife had serious potential. first half of the movie i loved until they just started jackin off all the old actors, even with a morally repulsive cgi cameo of Harold Ramis.

if the entire point is just LOOK AT THE ORIGINAL ACTORS! DO YOU REMEMBER THE THING? THEY'RE DOIN THE THING! then why am i not just watching the far more fun original ?

Glad they have a fresh villain instead of just reusing Vigo as the villain again according to the early rumors I've heard before the teaser trailer to this movie appeared. As much as I like seeing Walter Peck again, part of me also wanted a freah new human heel character to be a jerk and a second side antagonist to the Ghostbusters, like they did with Jack Hardemeyer for GB2. 

I tought it would've been cool if Winston restarted the GB business and then a new heel character plagiarized the GB tech to start their own knuckoff GB company for their own very selfish greed and profit driven interest. I think there was a character and angle like that in the comics.

textDisplay:
It looks more interesting than Afterlife, but there are still way too many memberberries in the trailer alone, I'm surprised more people aren't calling this out. Too many characters, lines and themes from the original are reused here. Library ghost? Check. Variation of "who you gonna call"? Check. "Ghostbusters, whaddya want?" Check. Lines from the song? Check. Getting slimed by Slimer? Check. The lion statue? Check. Walter Peck? Check. 

They're banking a little too hard on nostalgia, of which I've been totally depleted in the last ten years, so I do hope they've got a lot more than this to offer. Some of the new lines are funnier this time, and I love the idea of a drone trap, but I'm gonna need to see some more innovation if this is more than just a rehash.
textOriginal:
It looks more interesting than Afterlife, but there are still way too many memberberries in the trailer alone, I'm surprised more people aren't calling this out. Too many characters, lines and themes from the original are reused here. Library ghost? Check. Variation of "who you gonna call"? Check. "Ghostbusters, whaddya want?" Check. Lines from the song? Check. Getting slimed by Slimer? Check. The lion statue? Check. Walter Peck? Check. 

They're banking a little too hard on nostalgia, of which I've been totally depleted in the last ten years, so I do hope they've got a lot more than this to offer. Some of the new lines are funnier this time, and I love the idea of a drone trap, but I'm gonna need to see some more innovation if this is more than just a rehash.

textDisplay:
Hollywood is trying really hard now. Great they didn't lose their touch and showed the ghost, and lengthy bits of the film instead of keeping it a mystery as to the cause of it all. Great movie though 8/10, closer to a 9 than a 7.

Honestly though, no hate. If I did a trailer layout, I'd show the bits that would make people reminisce, a couple lines, but film-makers need to be more mysterious about their main content(such as protagonists, revealing the original cast to be a part of it, but give a glimpse or a hint that they might be in, let alone the imbd cast list that could be looked up) given to a movie. The mystery would give a greater pull.
textOriginal:
Hollywood is trying really hard now. Great they didn't lose their touch and showed the ghost, and lengthy bits of the film instead of keeping it a mystery as to the cause of it all. Great movie though 8/10, closer to a 9 than a 7.

Honestly though, no hate. If I did a trailer layout, I'd show the bits that would make people reminisce, a couple lines, but film-makers need to be more mysterious about their main content(such as protagonists, revealing the original cast to be a part of it, but give a glimpse or a hint that they might be in, let alone the imbd cast list that could be looked up) given to a movie. The mystery would give a greater pull.

textDisplay: I'm still not 100% sold on this movie, but I am glad to give it a try. After all, I quite enjoyed the previous one, it was a competent and heartfelt sequel to the originals, despite some flaws. My only gripe with this one is the "too much of everything" effect... you get the legacy characters, the callbacks to the original, the new team, the cartoon references... tone and pacing will probably be all over the place, if people at the helm of this project haven't find the right balance. Oh well, we'll find out very soon!🤞🏻
textOriginal: I'm still not 100% sold on this movie, but I am glad to give it a try. After all, I quite enjoyed the previous one, it was a competent and heartfelt sequel to the originals, despite some flaws. My only gripe with this one is the "too much of everything" effect... you get the legacy characters, the callbacks to the original, the new team, the cartoon references... tone and pacing will probably be all over the place, if people at the helm of this project haven't find the right balance. Oh well, we'll find out very soon!🤞🏻

textDisplay: I thought William Atherton was dead. But the one thing I hate about all these superhero films is the fact that politicians like to blame the hero for bringing War to the world when the truth is they came here to save the world they came here for a purpose to live among you guys to live peaceful lives and yet somebody from their world who has the opposite of their goals comes to Earth and starts a fight for example Superman came to Earth because his people were doomed he came to Earth and he was raised by a farmer and his wife they taught him the value of good they taught him how to Value human life they also talking how to fight against his own evils and yes even if there was a threat coming to Earth he and people like him will always be there every day to combat it but at the same time live normal lives and government officials have no jurisdiction in that matter to blame them for saving the world they should be kissing their feet because if they didn't exist the world would have been doomed. All I'm saying is we need to have a world where heroes are honored not blamed cherished not hated because they will go above and beyond even if it means sacrificing themselves to saving the world and they will do it over and over again to ensure that there will be peace they may not live long enough to see it but they hope that somebody will follow their example and that's exactly what the Ghostbusters are they are out there trying to save the world to maintaining the balance between the living and the dead Realms of the universe

textDisplay: ​user-cf6kr4ry8d So much hate for such a harmless film. 🤣Utter nonsense btw and putting it in CAPITALS doesn't make it any less so. It has a very simple plot which tied in the old and the new in a tasteful way. They had to deal with the fact Harold Ramis had passed away so handled it delicately and in an endearing way by making his family the focal point. The memberberries were a little in your face I'll hand you that point - but I don't think it was enough to get upset about and many of them tied in with the story and weren't just shameless  "Remember this?" moments with no context.
textOriginal: ​user-cf6kr4ry8d So much hate for such a harmless film. 🤣Utter nonsense btw and putting it in CAPITALS doesn't make it any less so. It has a very simple plot which tied in the old and the new in a tasteful way. They had to deal with the fact Harold Ramis had passed away so handled it delicately and in an endearing way by making his family the focal point. The memberberries were a little in your face I'll hand you that point - but I don't think it was enough to get upset about and many of them tied in with the story and weren't just shameless  "Remember this?" moments with no context.

textDisplay:
Love that the original Ghostbusters are returning, and it looks like, playing a BIG part in this one!!👻  Also love, the return of Grooberson (Rudd), and the return of Janine (Potts) and Peck (Atherton).  
I'm still not crazy about the kids from Afterlife, Phoebe (Grace) & Trevor (Wolfhard), but this one looks more like a grown-up movie, so maybe their parts are smaller, or they grew out of the annoying stuff from, Afterlife.  Podcast (Kim) was great in Afterlife, but I didn't see him in this trailer.  Hoping he's in this one, somewhere.  The story looks fantastic!  I'm really excited to discover what they came up with.  Should be a good ride, &  great part of summer 2024!
textOriginal:
Love that the original Ghostbusters are returning, and it looks like, playing a BIG part in this one!!👻  Also love, the return of Grooberson (Rudd), and the return of Janine (Potts) and Peck (Atherton).  
I'm still not crazy about the kids from Afterlife, Phoebe (Grace) & Trevor (Wolfhard), but this one looks more like a grown-up movie, so maybe their parts are smaller, or they grew out of the annoying stuff from, Afterlife.  Podcast (Kim) was great in Afterlife, but I didn't see him in this trailer.  Hoping he's in this one, somewhere.  The story looks fantastic!  I'm really excited to discover what they came up with.  Should be a good ride, &  great part of summer 2024!

textDisplay: The problem is that the original characters are old, and the new ones are not anywhere near their league. Bill Murray and Dan Ackroyd were two of the most famous comedians in the world when the original was made. And you had Rick Moranis also from sketch comedy on SCTV. And Ramis was a comedy writer. The new characters needed to fit that description. Not be lame actors. And the relentless forced feminism is nauseating as always. Every movie being progressive propaganda is killing Hollywood. Is there any chance this has nothing to do with “global warming”? That would be surprise.

textDisplay:
"I've waited 40 years for this." Peck really rubbing it in, especially for any of us that was born the same year the movie came out! I was born October 1984 and I'll be hitting the big 4-0 this year, and I'll be doing it alongside my most favorite movie!

Ghostbusters hits something special inside me, its a connection to something special that I hold tender in my heart. As the years go on, and as I age the same way my movie heros do, I'll still value this connection, and celebrate it in as many ways as I possibly can. Life requires us to be an adult sometimes, but I think it's obvious what the preference is right? Being a kid at heart can really make the hardships of adult life a lot easier.
textOriginal:
"I've waited 40 years for this." Peck really rubbing it in, especially for any of us that was born the same year the movie came out! I was born October 1984 and I'll be hitting the big 4-0 this year, and I'll be doing it alongside my most favorite movie!

Ghostbusters hits something special inside me, its a connection to something special that I hold tender in my heart. As the years go on, and as I age the same way my movie heros do, I'll still value this connection, and celebrate it in as many ways as I possibly can. Life requires us to be an adult sometimes, but I think it's obvious what the preference is right? Being a kid at heart can really make the hardships of adult life a lot easier.

textDisplay: alright, I'm ready to get off the nostalgia train. The humor of the original movie is primarily based on an irreverence of if its own material, but now we're treating it like some kind of holy writ. I'd like to see some blockbusters that have something to offer besides showing me a thing I saw a long time ago. Or, if you absolutely have to make a sequel or reboot, go somewhere new with it and don't be so fucking precious about the source material. Archeologists of the future are going to dig these movies up and think sci-fi was a major religion.
textOriginal: alright, I'm ready to get off the nostalgia train. The humor of the original movie is primarily based on an irreverence of if its own material, but now we're treating it like some kind of holy writ. I'd like to see some blockbusters that have something to offer besides showing me a thing I saw a long time ago. Or, if you absolutely have to make a sequel or reboot, go somewhere new with it and don't be so fucking precious about the source material. Archeologists of the future are going to dig these movies up and think sci-fi was a major religion.

textDisplay: So, if Walter Peck is actually the mayor – – and judging by his office it looks like he might be – – maybe he has been using his political clout to deny and convince others that the Ghostbusters weren’t the heroes they are.  There is an old saying from Joseph Goebbels (Nazi propaganda minister) that states, “If you repeat a lie often enough, it becomes the truth.”  Back in 1984, with very little recording devices being in the hands of every day people, it would be easy to convince people the ghosts weren’t real or imply the Marshmallow Man was a hallucination (Peck loves “nerve gas” as his excuse).  People don’t want to come across as crazy.  This could explain why no one really knows who the Ghostbusters really are now.  Just a speculation.
textOriginal: So, if Walter Peck is actually the mayor – – and judging by his office it looks like he might be – – maybe he has been using his political clout to deny and convince others that the Ghostbusters weren’t the heroes they are.  There is an old saying from Joseph Goebbels (Nazi propaganda minister) that states, “If you repeat a lie often enough, it becomes the truth.”  Back in 1984, with very little recording devices being in the hands of every day people, it would be easy to convince people the ghosts weren’t real or imply the Marshmallow Man was a hallucination (Peck loves “nerve gas” as his excuse).  People don’t want to come across as crazy.  This could explain why no one really knows who the Ghostbusters really are now.  Just a speculation.

although im probably one of very few who really liked ghostbusters 2 😅

textDisplay:
I have been obsessed with Ghostbusters my whole life. To see it return and continue has always been wishful thinking until now.

However, something feels....off? The returning OG actors feel flat, like it's merely a contractual obligation, then invested progression of their character arcs. Maybe thats just Bill Murray.

I just don't get why, like afterlife, they are just rehashing the same ideas from the first 2. The lions, the library, the problems with authority. Heck, they won't even take it away from NYC (yes I know afterlife was but that was still just a farm) what about a global infestation or something. Huge earthly problems at stake. There is so much they cant so with this concept and they just reuse 30 year old assets
textOriginal:
I have been obsessed with Ghostbusters my whole life. To see it return and continue has always been wishful thinking until now.

However, something feels....off? The returning OG actors feel flat, like it's merely a contractual obligation, then invested progression of their character arcs. Maybe thats just Bill Murray.

I just don't get why, like afterlife, they are just rehashing the same ideas from the first 2. The lions, the library, the problems with authority. Heck, they won't even take it away from NYC (yes I know afterlife was but that was still just a farm) what about a global infestation or something. Huge earthly problems at stake. There is so much they cant so with this concept and they just reuse 30 year old assets

textDisplay:
@thomasjones4570 Actually, if I recall, while the large-scale disasters were in front of crowds, and the crowds saw the guys go to "ground zero", the actual final battles took place in private.  No one else saw Gozer up there on the top of the building.  All the crowds saw was the guys go in, a giant marshmallow creature appear, walk to the same place, then explode, and the guys step out covered in melted mallow.  No actual eyewitness of the extermination or clear definition of what exactly Gozer or the marshmallow man are, or proof that they weren't a product of the guy's "special effects".  Similar in the second, everyone saw the things going on outside, but they didn't see the actual Big Bad or his destruction/sealing.

So yeah, no eyewitnesses.  Aside from two people who were unconscious and whose memories included themselves turning into dogs.
textOriginal:
@thomasjones4570 Actually, if I recall, while the large-scale disasters were in front of crowds, and the crowds saw the guys go to "ground zero", the actual final battles took place in private.  No one else saw Gozer up there on the top of the building.  All the crowds saw was the guys go in, a giant marshmallow creature appear, walk to the same place, then explode, and the guys step out covered in melted mallow.  No actual eyewitness of the extermination or clear definition of what exactly Gozer or the marshmallow man are, or proof that they weren't a product of the guy's "special effects".  Similar in the second, everyone saw the things going on outside, but they didn't see the actual Big Bad or his destruction/sealing.

So yeah, no eyewitnesses.  Aside from two people who were unconscious and whose memories included themselves turning into dogs.

textDisplay:
Honest question, and it's not a big deal, but holy DAMN does it annoy me...

What is it with movie trailers starting with a preview of what we're about to watch? "THE TRAILER STARTS NOW!" Well, duh. That's what's supposed to happen when you click on the thumbnail. That dumb announcement doesn't make me excited to watch the trailer, BECAUSE I'M ALREADY WATCHING IT! 

No, seriously... who is that for? What is the point? It's the thumbnail that gets me excited to watch the trailer. In fact, the stupid little pre-show makes me realize that I'm about to see the same clips again in a minute! You're making a movie trailer that uses the same footage twice! LAME! 

So, my question is... why? Why can't you just LET THE TRAILER START NOW instead of announcing that you're going to start the trailer? JUST DO IT!
textOriginal:
Honest question, and it's not a big deal, but holy DAMN does it annoy me...

What is it with movie trailers starting with a preview of what we're about to watch? "THE TRAILER STARTS NOW!" Well, duh. That's what's supposed to happen when you click on the thumbnail. That dumb announcement doesn't make me excited to watch the trailer, BECAUSE I'M ALREADY WATCHING IT! 

No, seriously... who is that for? What is the point? It's the thumbnail that gets me excited to watch the trailer. In fact, the stupid little pre-show makes me realize that I'm about to see the same clips again in a minute! You're making a movie trailer that uses the same footage twice! LAME! 

So, my question is... why? Why can't you just LET THE TRAILER START NOW instead of announcing that you're going to start the trailer? JUST DO IT!

textDisplay: @JB0143RP is Finn playing himself or a character? Then his real age doesn't apply here.  And McKenna IS a kid. At least compared to the adults who played actual Ghostbusters in the original movies.  Peter, Ray, Egon.. those guys had PHDs.  Years of studying and working long before they ever lifted a proton pack. And Winston didn't just pick it up and magically knew how to operate it. He was still trained.  Like I said, feels very Spy Kids.  But again, it's a nitpick and will not stop me from paying to see this still.  At the end of the day, it's nice to see the guys back again.

textDisplay: @grrayfox2300 I understand what you are saying, but I think McKennas character is good even if young. Finns character is lacking any depth and isn't great. But the mom and his girlfriend are worse, total throw away characters.
textOriginal: @grrayfox2300 I understand what you are saying, but I think McKennas character is good even if young. Finns character is lacking any depth and isn't great. But the mom and his girlfriend are worse, total throw away characters.

textDisplay: @JB0143RP for sure. Don't get me wrong, McKenna is great. Finn is talented as well. I agree about the throwaway characters. I'm not opposed to some good exposition that fleshes out these characters more. Maybe this movie will answer some of that. I really think the potential to build on this universe is there. I just don't want them underestimating the audience's ability to connect to well written characters. That's what made the originals so great. You could have put those guys into any absurd paranormal storyline and it still would have been fine.
textOriginal: @JB0143RP for sure. Don't get me wrong, McKenna is great. Finn is talented as well. I agree about the throwaway characters. I'm not opposed to some good exposition that fleshes out these characters more. Maybe this movie will answer some of that. I really think the potential to build on this universe is there. I just don't want them underestimating the audience's ability to connect to well written characters. That's what made the originals so great. You could have put those guys into any absurd paranormal storyline and it still would have been fine.

textDisplay:
@classicthought266 A couple corrections:

-Every Ghostbusters has women in it, I assume you mean Ghostbusters: Answer the Call


-It's spelled canon.

-Canon is not the same thing as Continuity.
Ghostbusters: Answer the Call is canon because it's an official Sony-owned Ghostbusters project. Real Ghostbusters and Extreme Ghostbusters are canon, too. The comics are canon, too.

-They're in a separate continuity from the original Ghostbusters, except for the time they met the original Ghostbusters, meaning they exist within the same multiverse

-The individual film's continuity doesn't change the fact that all 4 are great, soon to be 5.
textOriginal:
@classicthought266 A couple corrections:

-Every Ghostbusters has women in it, I assume you mean Ghostbusters: Answer the Call


-It's spelled canon.

-Canon is not the same thing as Continuity.
Ghostbusters: Answer the Call is canon because it's an official Sony-owned Ghostbusters project. Real Ghostbusters and Extreme Ghostbusters are canon, too. The comics are canon, too.

-They're in a separate continuity from the original Ghostbusters, except for the time they met the original Ghostbusters, meaning they exist within the same multiverse

-The individual film's continuity doesn't change the fact that all 4 are great, soon to be 5.

textDisplay:
Can we stop with franchises? This not the 7th art. This is just content… Product they are giving us to buy there sh.. And I don’t want to be only entertained, I want to be moved, emotionally and intellectually. I want to laugh not to a joke but to a situation, because of charismatic characters. Not because I saw that funny actor in another movie. 
I want a director vision express itself through an ambitious narrative. Hollywood is depressing. 
I’m sure everybody worked with all their heart on this but this is not what I want. Not anymore… 
Not every franchises needs to be a Marvel cinematic universe. Producers should look at what people think about marvelisation I the industry. 
We are not sheep !

textDisplay:
They lost me almost immediately, the moment that flashback to the first movie was on screen I checked out. Still trying to ride the success of the original instead of a new direction. 

Also the plot looks a bit too big, I always saw the GB as plumbers and handymen, average New York blue collar workers who just happened to catch ghosts not world saving heroes. 

And yes i do see the contradiction of these 2 statements but one is about the spirit of the original and the other is about them straight up using the original.
textOriginal:
They lost me almost immediately, the moment that flashback to the first movie was on screen I checked out. Still trying to ride the success of the original instead of a new direction. 

Also the plot looks a bit too big, I always saw the GB as plumbers and handymen, average New York blue collar workers who just happened to catch ghosts not world saving heroes. 

And yes i do see the contradiction of these 2 statements but one is about the spirit of the original and the other is about them straight up using the original.

textDisplay:
Sweet. 5 year old me is ecstatic haha. This looks like some good old fun. Like how movies used to be. I honestly can't think of a movie that was memorable since the internet and streaming became the norm.  Maybe some of the Marvel movies up until Endgame. But even those had a predictable formula by the time the first Guardians movie came out. 
I'm sick of movies that take place during either of the world wars and crime/mobster movies are always "let's see how long the main character lasts until they get arrested or killed". Zombie movies are all literally the same thing every time. Stupid teen movies are just fart and sex jokes. 
Horror movies have become more gross than actually scary.
A good old sci-fi adventure with a little comedy sounds like a fun time to this old nerd haha.
textOriginal:
Sweet. 5 year old me is ecstatic haha. This looks like some good old fun. Like how movies used to be. I honestly can't think of a movie that was memorable since the internet and streaming became the norm.  Maybe some of the Marvel movies up until Endgame. But even those had a predictable formula by the time the first Guardians movie came out. 
I'm sick of movies that take place during either of the world wars and crime/mobster movies are always "let's see how long the main character lasts until they get arrested or killed". Zombie movies are all literally the same thing every time. Stupid teen movies are just fart and sex jokes. 
Horror movies have become more gross than actually scary.
A good old sci-fi adventure with a little comedy sounds like a fun time to this old nerd haha.

textDisplay: As soon as Bill Murray appeared the charisma levels jumped up. He just on a different level and has that presence that none of the other (new) cast have. I like Paul Rudd, but the original actors (including Sigourney Weaver and Rick Moranis), had personalities, chemistry and comedic timing/delivery that won't be bettered. This is just a different era of the franchise (just like happened to Star Wars). If you don't have a legacy love and attachment to the originals and their characters (because they were such a part of your younger years) then these new stories and cast members are just easier to 'accept'. Hope it's a success.
textOriginal: As soon as Bill Murray appeared the charisma levels jumped up. He just on a different level and has that presence that none of the other (new) cast have. I like Paul Rudd, but the original actors (including Sigourney Weaver and Rick Moranis), had personalities, chemistry and comedic timing/delivery that won't be bettered. This is just a different era of the franchise (just like happened to Star Wars). If you don't have a legacy love and attachment to the originals and their characters (because they were such a part of your younger years) then these new stories and cast members are just easier to 'accept'. Hope it's a success.

textDisplay:
While I'm curious of the movie, I  can't let it go to see the weak "GB Team". A bunch of kids, a non-science knowing, a non military trained Mom and a school teacher....
It just don't feel like Ghostbusters should. The ONLY kid wanted was Phoebe Spengler, she was/is perfect to be "The Next" Ghostbuster, but her lazy uneducated brother?
her pal? his brother's girld friend? What worked in ONE FILM can't be repeated with a bunch of kids saving the day.  It doesen't hold any way you see it.

Heck when I was a little kid, I imagined being a Ghostbuster demanded to be a knowledgble adult = what I wanted to be in my fantasy. Not being a super kid with highly dangerous high-tech equipment. 

Come on here, why was it so hard to make a solid new team under training by Dr. Ray Stanz and Winston Zeddmore with or with-out Dr. Peter Venkman and only having Phoebe Spengler onboard as the "super promising tallent" in education of paranormal and science knowledge? The rest are a mix of ADULTS with relatble academic background and/or Military background. That is how it should been here.

textDisplay:
Yep and it appears lots more inspiration I see was taken from the 2008 video game.  Such as the library appearance is heavily inspired by the video game.  Then the lion statue that is also loosely inspired as well by the game.  That ghost being captured by the drone trap also appears to be a heavily inspired animator from the library chapter of the video game.

Can't wait to see this in theaters and see what other video game inspirations are taken as well.
textOriginal:
Yep and it appears lots more inspiration I see was taken from the 2008 video game.  Such as the library appearance is heavily inspired by the video game.  Then the lion statue that is also loosely inspired as well by the game.  That ghost being captured by the drone trap also appears to be a heavily inspired animator from the library chapter of the video game.

Can't wait to see this in theaters and see what other video game inspirations are taken as well.
